title: Build Your $500 Emergency Fund First
body: Before anything else, save $500. This single buffer covers most unexpected expenses: car repair, medical copay, textbooks. Save $10/week and you're there in under a year.

title: Internship Income Plan
body: If you earn $4,000 this summer, consider: 40% savings, 30% school expenses, 20% personal spending, 10% investing or exploring. Adjust as needed — but plan before you spend.

title: The $5 Trick for Overspending
body: Before any non-essential purchase over $20, wait 48 hours. If you still want it, buy it. You'll be surprised how often the impulse disappears.

title: Cancel One Subscription Today
body: Open your bank app. Find a subscription you forgot about. Cancel it. That $12–15/month is $144–180/year — enough to seed your emergency fund.

title: What Is a Roth IRA and Why You Should Start Now
body: A Roth IRA lets you invest after-tax dollars that grow tax-free. $50/month starting at 20 can become $150,000+ by 60 with average market returns. Open one at Fidelity or Schwab — no minimums.

title: Track One Week of Spending
body: Don't budget yet — just observe. Write down every purchase for 7 days. No judgment. The goal is to see your actual patterns before making changes.

title: Student Loan 101
body: Know the type of loan you have. Subsidized loans (Federal Direct) don't accrue interest while enrolled. Unsubsidized do. Understanding this before graduation can save thousands.

title: Build Credit the Safe Way
body: Get a secured credit card or become an authorized user on a parent's account. Use it for small purchases. Pay it in full every month. Good credit saves you money on everything later.

title: Campus Job Income Plan
body: Even 10 hours/week at $15/hr is $600/month. Before spending it, assign every dollar a purpose: $200 savings, $200 food, $100 personal, $100 emergency fund. Done.

title: Food Budget Without Starving
body: Food is the easiest category to overspend. Try a simple rule: 3 meals out per week max. Cook twice as much when you do cook. Meal prep Sunday is 30 min that saves $50+.
